comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

Review Summary by Qodo

Implement Jules question auto-answer with max-cycle enforcement and deduplication ✨ Enhancement 🧪 Tests

Walkthroughs

Description 
• Add awaiting_feedback status to Jules normalization and RunStatus
• Implement three new Temporal activities for question extraction and auto-answer
• Wire auto-answer sub-flow into MoonMind.AgentRun polling loop with guardrails
• Add 30+ unit tests covering status mapping, Pydantic models, transport, and workflow logic
Diagram
> ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Jules enters
AWAITING_USER_FEEDBACK"] -->|detect| B["list_activities
activity"] B -->|extract question| C["answer_question
activity"] C -->|LLM dispatch| D["send_message
activity"] D -->|resume| E["Jules continues
IN_PROGRESS"] F["Max cycles
exceeded?"] -->|yes| G["escalate to
intervention_requested"] F -->|no| E H["Dedup check
activity_id"] -->|already answered| I["skip LLM
continue polling"] H -->|new question| C ```

Code Review by Qodo

🐞 Bugs (6) 📘 Rule violations (2) 📎 Requirement gaps (0) 📐 Spec deviations (0)
1. Invalid env vars silently default 📘 Rule violation ⛯ Reliability
>
>Description >
> >
>JULES_MAX_AUTO_ANSWERS and JULES_AUTO_ANSWER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS parse failures are silently coerced
>to defaults instead of failing explicitly. This can hide misconfiguration and violate the required
>fail-fast behavior for unsupported runtime inputs.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py[R266-276]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/pull/810/files#diff-25be9c63a03b969cca0e0674994b1f3458ddb624de72ce5bba8cad459eca871dR266-R276) > >```diff >+ enabled_raw = os.environ.get("JULES_AUTO_ANSWER_ENABLED", "true").strip().lower() >+ enabled = enabled_raw not in ("false", "0", "no", "off") >+ try: >+ max_answers = int(os.environ.get("JULES_MAX_AUTO_ANSWERS", "3")) >+ except ValueError: >+ max_answers = 3 >+ runtime = os.environ.get("JULES_AUTO_ANSWER_RUNTIME", "llm").strip() or "llm" >+ try: >+ timeout = int(os.environ.get("JULES_AUTO_ANSWER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "300")) >+ except ValueError: >+ timeout = 300 >``` >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>PR Compliance ID 147364 requires unsupported runtime input values to cause an explicit failure
>rather than silent fallback. The new env-var parsing catches ValueError and falls back to defaults
>in both the Temporal activity and the runtime handler.
>
> > Rule 147364: [Fail fast on unsupported runtime input values](https://app.qodo.ai/rules/147364?state=active) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py[266-276]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py/#L266-L276) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1981-1991]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py/#L1981-L1991) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >## Issue description >Auto-answer configuration parsing silently defaults when env vars are invalid (non-integer), instead of failing fast. > >## Issue Context >Compliance requires unsupported runtime inputs to trigger an explicit failure path (exception / non-zero / clear error), not a hidden fallback. > >## Fix Focus Areas >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py[266-276] >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1981-1991] >``` > ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >
2. Unknown normalized_status coerced to running 📘 Rule violation ⛯ Reliability
>
>Description >
> >
>An unsupported normalized_status from the integration start result is coerced to running instead
>of failing or surfacing an explicit error. This hides invalid runtime input and can cause incorrect
>workflow behavior.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[R591-593]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/pull/810/files#diff-03f3b0435ee0ae6fcc8dbb6309cf35d957099600f25ed651d9a526092e3b9538R591-R593) > >```diff > status = handle_dict.get("normalized_status", "unknown") >- if status not in {"queued", "launching", "running", "awaiting_callback", "awaiting_approval", "intervention_requested", "collecting_results", "completed", "failed", "cancelled", "timed_out"}: >+ if status not in {"queued", "launching", "running", "awaiting_callback", "awaiting_feedback", "awaiting_approval", "intervention_requested", "collecting_results", "completed", "failed", "cancelled", "timed_out"}: > status = "running" >``` >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>PR Compliance ID 147364 requires fail-fast handling for unsupported runtime inputs. The workflow
>explicitly checks for an allow-list of statuses but then silently rewrites any unknown value to
>running rather than raising an error or returning a validation failure.
>
> > Rule 147364: [Fail fast on unsupported runtime input values](https://app.qodo.ai/rules/147364?state=active) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[591-593]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py/#L591-L593) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >## Issue description >`normalized_status` values outside the allow-list are silently coerced to `running`. > >## Issue Context >This is a runtime input coming from an external integration activity result. Compliance requires unsupported values to trigger an explicit failure rather than a hidden fallback. > >## Fix Focus Areas >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[591-593] >``` > ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >
3. Adapter missing new status 🐞 Bug ✓ Correctness
>
>Description >
> >
>JulesAgentAdapter indexes _JULES_TO_AGENT_RUN_STATUS with the normalized status from
>moonmind.schemas.jules_models.normalize_jules_status, but the new value "awaiting_feedback" is not
>in that map, causing a KeyError when Jules returns AWAITING_USER_FEEDBACK and breaking status
>polling/auto-answer.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[moonmind/schemas/jules_models.py[R19-25]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/pull/810/files#diff-93909748ab0ee1e80309ebd65afed72c0f0d023e905268fac89357ed96006e24R19-R25) > >```diff > _JULES_STATUS_MAP: dict[str, JulesNormalizedStatus] = { > "accepted": "queued", > "assigned": "queued", > # -- Actual Jules API State enum values (case-insensitive) -- > "awaiting_plan_approval": "running", >- "awaiting_user_feedback": "running", >+ "awaiting_user_feedback": "awaiting_feedback", > "blocked": "running", >``` >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>The PR changes Jules normalization to return "awaiting_feedback" for "awaiting_user_feedback".
>JulesAgentAdapter then maps that normalized value via _JULES_TO_AGENT_RUN_STATUS[normalized_status];
>since the mapping does not include "awaiting_feedback", status polling will raise KeyError when
>Jules enters AWAITING_USER_FEEDBACK.
>
> > [moonmind/schemas/jules_models.py[9-50]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/schemas/jules_models.py/#L9-L50) > [moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py[31-52]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py/#L31-L52) > [moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py[164-180]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py/#L164-L180) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >### Issue description >`moonmind.schemas.jules_models.normalize_jules_status()` can now return `"awaiting_feedback"`, but `JulesAgentAdapter` still assumes the normalized status is one of `{queued,running,succeeded,failed,canceled,unknown}`. This causes a `KeyError` when `do_status()` (and other paths) index `_JULES_TO_AGENT_RUN_STATUS[normalized_status]`. > >### Issue Context >This breaks the *status polling path* exactly when auto-answer is supposed to trigger. > >### Fix Focus Areas >- moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py[31-44] >- moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py[164-174] >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[676-681] > >### Suggested fix >1. Add an `"awaiting_feedback"` entry to `_JULES_TO_AGENT_RUN_STATUS` mapping to a stable workflow status (likely `"awaiting_feedback"`). >2. Ensure any downstream status validators/allowed sets accept `"awaiting_feedback"` (e.g., if there are enums/validators for AgentRunStatus). >``` > ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >
View more (4)
4. Activities not in catalog 🐞 Bug ⛯ Reliability
>
>Description >
> >
>AgentRun executes integration.jules.list_activities/answer_question/get_auto_answer_config, but
>build_worker_activity_bindings only registers activity types present in the Temporal activity
>catalog; these new activity types are missing there, so integrations workers won’t bind/register
>them and workflow execution will fail at runtime.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[R682-729]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/pull/810/files#diff-03f3b0435ee0ae6fcc8dbb6309cf35d957099600f25ed651d9a526092e3b9538R682-R729) > >```diff >+ # Read config via activity (determinism-safe) >+ auto_answer_config = await workflow.execute_activity( >+ "integration.jules.get_auto_answer_config", >+ [], >+ task_queue=INTEGRATIONS_TASK_QUEUE, >+ start_to_close_timeout=timedelta(seconds=10), >+ cancellation_type=ActivityCancellationType.TRY_CANCEL, >+ ) >+ aa_enabled = auto_answer_config.get("enabled", True) if isinstance(auto_answer_config, dict) else True >+ aa_max = auto_answer_config.get("max_answers", 3) if isinstance(auto_answer_config, dict) else 3 >+ >+ if not aa_enabled or self._auto_answer_count >= aa_max: >+ # Opt-out or max cycles exhausted → escalate >+ self.run_status = "intervention_requested" >+ workflow.logger.warning( >+ "Jules auto-answer %s for session %s (count=%d, max=%d)", >+ "disabled" if not aa_enabled else "exhausted", >+ self.run_id, >+ self._auto_answer_count, >+ aa_max, >+ ) >+ break >+ >+ # Extract the question >+ activities_result = await workflow.execute_activity( >+ "integration.jules.list_activities", >+ self.run_id, >+ task_queue=INTEGRATIONS_TASK_QUEUE, >+ start_to_close_timeout=INTEGRATIONS_STATUS_TIMEOUT, >+ cancellation_type=ActivityCancellationType.TRY_CANCEL, >+ ) >+ >+ act_id = activities_result.get("activityId") if isinstance(activities_result, dict) else None >+ question = activities_result.get("latestAgentQuestion") if isinstance(activities_result, dict) else None >+ >+ if question and act_id and act_id not in self._answered_activity_ids: >+ # Dispatch question-answer cycle >+ task_context = request.instruction_ref or request.agent_id or "" >+ answer_result = await workflow.execute_activity( >+ "integration.jules.answer_question", >+ { >+ "session_id": self.run_id, >+ "question": question, >+ "task_context": task_context, >+ }, >+ task_queue=INTEGRATIONS_TASK_QUEUE, >+ start_to_close_timeout=INTEGRATIONS_ACTIVITY_TIMEOUT, >+ cancellation_type=ActivityCancellationType.TRY_CANCEL, >``` >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>The worker runtime binds activities by iterating catalog.activities (not by importing
>moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/*.py). The default catalog currently lists existing Jules
>activities but does not define the three new activity types, so build_activity_bindings() will
>never bind a handler for them and the Temporal worker will not register them.
>
> > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[676-753]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py/#L676-L753) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py[459-512]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py/#L459-L512) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[2990-3050]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py/#L2990-L3050) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/worker_entrypoint.py[38-52]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/worker_entrypoint.py/#L38-L52) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >### Issue description >The new Jules auto-answer activities are invoked from the workflow, but they are not declared in the Temporal activity catalog used to build and register worker activity bindings. As a result, integrations workers will not register these activities and Temporal will fail the workflow when it tries to schedule them. > >### Issue Context >`build_worker_activity_bindings()` uses `build_default_activity_catalog()` and then `build_activity_bindings()` which iterates `catalog.activities` to build the actual registered activity list. > >### Fix Focus Areas >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py[430-513] >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[176-208] >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[682-729] > >### Suggested fix >1. In `build_default_activity_catalog()` add `TemporalActivityDefinition(...)` entries for: > - `integration.jules.list_activities` > - `integration.jules.answer_question` > - `integration.jules.get_auto_answer_config` > Use `cfg.activity_integrations_task_queue`, `INTEGRATIONS_FLEET`, and appropriate timeouts/retries. >2. Ensure `_ACTIVITY_HANDLER_ATTRS` already maps these activity types to runtime handlers (it does in this PR). >``` > ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >
5. list_activities payload mismatch 🐞 Bug ✓ Correctness
>
>Description >
> >
>MoonMind.AgentRun calls integration.jules.list_activities with a raw string session id, but the
>TemporalActivityRuntime handler signature is keyword-only session_id, and the activity wrapper
>only expands mappings into kwargs; this results in a TypeError (missing required session_id) at
>runtime.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[R705-712]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/pull/810/files#diff-03f3b0435ee0ae6fcc8dbb6309cf35d957099600f25ed651d9a526092e3b9538R705-R712) > >```diff >+ # Extract the question >+ activities_result = await workflow.execute_activity( >+ "integration.jules.list_activities", >+ self.run_id, >+ task_queue=INTEGRATIONS_TASK_QUEUE, >+ start_to_close_timeout=INTEGRATIONS_STATUS_TIMEOUT, >+ cancellation_type=ActivityCancellationType.TRY_CANCEL, >+ ) >``` >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>The bound activity function always receives a single request argument. The wrapper expands
>mappings as keyword args, but for non-mapping inputs it calls the handler with request=<value>;
>since integration_jules_list_activities requires kw-only session_id, passing a string cannot
>satisfy it and will fail with a missing required argument.
>
> > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[705-712]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py/#L705-L712) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1918-1931]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py/#L1918-L1931) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[2929-2952]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py/#L2929-L2952) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >```` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >### Issue description >`workflow.execute_activity("integration.jules.list_activities", self.run_id, ...)` passes a string. The runtime activity wrapper only expands `Mapping` inputs into keyword args; otherwise it forwards `request=<value>`. Because `integration_jules_list_activities()` requires `session_id` as a keyword-only arg, this call will raise a `TypeError`. > >### Issue Context >This affects the integrations worker runtime path (the one actually registered by `worker_entrypoint.py`). > >### Fix Focus Areas >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[705-712] >- moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1918-1931] > >### Suggested fix >Change the workflow invocation to pass a JSON object: >```py >activities_result = await workflow.execute_activity( > "integration.jules.list_activities", > {"session_id": self.run_id}, > ... >) >``` >Optionally make the runtime handler accept a positional `request` and coerce it, but aligning on mapping payloads is consistent with other integration activities (e.g., `integration.jules.status`). >```` > ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >
6. Sends prompt not answer 🐞 Bug ✓ Correctness
>
>Description >
> >
>integration.jules.answer_question constructs an LLM instruction prompt and sends it to Jules as the
>"answer" without performing any LLM dispatch, so Jules receives meta-instructions rather than an
>actual clarification response.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py[R168-201]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/pull/810/files#diff-25be9c63a03b969cca0e0674994b1f3458ddb624de72ce5bba8cad459eca871dR168-R201) > >```diff >+ # Build the clarification prompt for the LLM >+ prompt_parts = [ >+ "You are answering a clarification question from Jules (an AI coding agent).", >+ ] >+ if task_context: >+ prompt_parts.append(f"Task context: {task_context}") >+ prompt_parts.extend([ >+ "", >+ f"Jules's question:\n{question}", >+ "", >+ "Provide a concise, actionable answer. If the question asks about a preference or", >+ "choice, choose the most reasonable default and explain your reasoning briefly.", >+ "Do not ask follow-up questions.", >+ ]) >+ clarification_prompt = "\n".join(prompt_parts) >+ >+ # For the default LLM path, we use a simple approach: >+ # Generate a reasonable answer based on the prompt context. >+ # In production, this would dispatch to mm.activity.llm.complete or >+ # a managed agent runtime. For now, we send the constructed prompt >+ # directly as the answer since the workflow layer will handle >+ # the actual LLM dispatch. >+ answer = clarification_prompt >+ >+ # Send the answer back to Jules >+ client = _build_client() >+ try: >+ await client.send_message( >+ JulesSendMessageRequest( >+ session_id=session_id, >+ prompt=answer, >+ ) >+ ) >+ return {"answered": True, "answer": answer, "error": None} >``` >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>Both the Temporal activities module and the runtime handler build a prompt asking an LLM to answer,
>but then directly use that prompt as the message sent back to Jules. The feature spec in-repo
>requires dispatching the extracted question to an LLM and then sending the *generated* answer back
>(and to do so via the existing send_message activity).
>
> > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py[168-201]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py/#L168-L201) > [moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1932-1966]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py/#L1932-L1966) > [specs/094-jules-auto-answer/spec.md[95-106]](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/8c9237cc5d68584927831276acc2a8205293626d/specs/094-jules-auto-answer/spec.md/#L95-L106) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >### Issue description >The auto-answer path does not actually generate an answer; it sends an LLM instruction prompt back to Jules. ## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request significantly enhances the Jules integration by automating the response to Jules's clarification questions. Previously, Jules workflows would halt when awaiting user feedback, necessitating manual intervention. Now, the system intelligently detects these questions, generates an LLM-powered answer, and relays it back to Jules, thereby streamlining workflow execution and reducing the need for human oversight. The implementation includes robust guardrails such as configurable answer limits, prevention of duplicate answers, and an easy opt-out option, ensuring controlled and efficient operation. ### Highlights * **Jules Auto-Answer Feature**: Introduced an automatic question-answering mechanism for Jules when it enters the 'AWAITING_USER_FEEDBACK' state, preventing workflow stalls. * **Status Normalization & Models**: Added 'awaiting_feedback' as a new normalized status and implemented new Pydantic models (`JulesAgentMessage`, `JulesActivity`, `JulesListActivitiesResult`) for parsing Jules API activities. * **New Temporal Activities**: Created and registered three new Temporal activities: `integration.jules.list_activities` (to extract questions), `integration.jules.answer_question` (to orchestrate the LLM-based answer cycle), and `integration.jules.get_auto_answer_config` (to read environment variables). * **Workflow Orchestration & Guardrails**: Integrated the auto-answer logic into the `MoonMind.AgentRun` polling loop, complete with guardrails for maximum answer cycles, question deduplication, and an opt-out mechanism via environment variables. * **Documentation & Specification**: Provided comprehensive documentation for the new feature, including updates to `JulesClientAdapter.md` and a full suite of specification documents (spec, plan, data model, traceability, etc.). * **Test Coverage**: Added extensive unit tests covering status normalization, Pydantic models, client transport, new activities, and the core workflow auto-answer logic. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**

Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. ## Unused import Import of 'JulesListActivitiesResult' is not used. --- In general, unused import warnings are fixed by either removing the unused symbol from the import statement or starting to use it in the code. To avoid changing existing behavior, the best approach is to remove only the specific identifier that is confirmed unused and leave all other imports intact.

In this file, the problematic line is the multi-name import from moonmind.schemas.jules_models. The static analysis tool reports that JulesListActivitiesResult is unused, and within the visible snippet there is no reference to it. To fix this without impacting any other behavior, adjust that import so it only imports JulesIntegrationMergePRResult and JulesSendMessageRequest and omits JulesListActivitiesResult. No other code changes or new definitions are needed.

Concretely, in moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py, replace the three-line import block starting at line 21 that currently includes JulesListActivitiesResult with an import that only brings in the two remaining names.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The current implementation sends the clarification prompt intended for the LLM directly back to Jules as the answer. The code comment acknowledges this is a temporary measure. This placeholder logic is functionally incorrect for production and should be replaced with a call to an actual LLM activity (e.g., `mm.activity.llm.complete`) to generate a valid answer.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The logic for the new activities (`integration_jules_list_activities`, `integration_jules_answer_question`, `integration_jules_get_auto_answer_config`) is duplicated from `moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py`. This creates a significant maintainability issue, as any future changes will need to be made in two places.

To improve maintainability, this duplicated code should be refactored into a single source of truth. For example, the functions in `jules_activities.py` could be thin wrappers that instantiate `TemporalActivityBinding` and call the corresponding method, or a shared helper could contain the core logic.

Additionally, the duplicated `integration_jules_answer_question` implementation here has the same functional issue as its counterpart: it sends the LLM prompt as the answer instead of generating a real one.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a valuable feature to automatically answer questions from the Jules agent, preventing workflows from stalling. The changes are comprehensive, spanning schema, transport, activities, and workflow orchestration, and are well-documented and tested. I've identified a couple of high-severity issues that should be addressed. There is significant code duplication in the implementation of the new Temporal activities, which will impact maintainability. Additionally, the core `answer_question` activity currently uses placeholder logic and sends the LLM prompt back as the answer, which needs to be replaced with a proper LLM call for this feature to be functional.

1\. Invalid env vars silently default 📘 Rule violation ⛯ Reliability 
JULES_MAX_AUTO_ANSWERS and JULES_AUTO_ANSWER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS parse failures are silently coerced
to defaults instead of failing explicitly. This can hide misconfiguration and violate the required
fail-fast behavior for unsupported runtime inputs.
Agent Prompt ``` ## Issue description Auto-answer configuration parsing silently defaults when env vars are invalid (non-integer), instead of failing fast. ## Issue Context Compliance requires unsupported runtime inputs to trigger an explicit failure path (exception / non-zero / clear error), not a hidden fallback. ## Fix Focus Areas - moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/jules_activities.py[266-276] - moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1981-1991] ``` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools

2\. Unknown normalized_status coerced to running 📘 Rule violation ⛯ Reliability 
An unsupported normalized_status from the integration start result is coerced to running instead
of failing or surfacing an explicit error. This hides invalid runtime input and can cause incorrect
workflow behavior.
Agent Prompt ``` ## Issue description `normalized_status` values outside the allow-list are silently coerced to `running`. ## Issue Context This is a runtime input coming from an external integration activity result. Compliance requires unsupported values to trigger an explicit failure rather than a hidden fallback. ## Fix Focus Areas - moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[591-593] ``` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools

3\. Adapter missing new status 🐞 Bug ✓ Correctness 
JulesAgentAdapter indexes _JULES_TO_AGENT_RUN_STATUS with the normalized status from
moonmind.schemas.jules_models.normalize_jules_status, but the new value "awaiting_feedback" is not
in that map, causing a KeyError when Jules returns AWAITING_USER_FEEDBACK and breaking status
polling/auto-answer.
Agent Prompt ``` ### Issue description `moonmind.schemas.jules_models.normalize_jules_status()` can now return `"awaiting_feedback"`, but `JulesAgentAdapter` still assumes the normalized status is one of `{queued,running,succeeded,failed,canceled,unknown}`. This causes a `KeyError` when `do_status()` (and other paths) index `_JULES_TO_AGENT_RUN_STATUS[normalized_status]`. ### Issue Context This breaks the *status polling path* exactly when auto-answer is supposed to trigger. ### Fix Focus Areas - moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py[31-44] - moonmind/workflows/adapters/jules_agent_adapter.py[164-174] - moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[676-681] ### Suggested fix 1. Add an `"awaiting_feedback"` entry to `_JULES_TO_AGENT_RUN_STATUS` mapping to a stable workflow status (likely `"awaiting_feedback"`). 2. Ensure any downstream status validators/allowed sets accept `"awaiting_feedback"` (e.g., if there are enums/validators for AgentRunStatus). ``` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools

4\. Activities not in catalog 🐞 Bug ⛯ Reliability 
AgentRun executes integration.jules.list_activities/answer_question/get_auto_answer_config, but
build_worker_activity_bindings only registers activity types present in the Temporal activity
catalog; these new activity types are missing there, so integrations workers won’t bind/register
them and workflow execution will fail at runtime.
Agent Prompt ``` ### Issue description The new Jules auto-answer activities are invoked from the workflow, but they are not declared in the Temporal activity catalog used to build and register worker activity bindings. As a result, integrations workers will not register these activities and Temporal will fail the workflow when it tries to schedule them. ### Issue Context `build_worker_activity_bindings()` uses `build_default_activity_catalog()` and then `build_activity_bindings()` which iterates `catalog.activities` to build the actual registered activity list. ### Fix Focus Areas - moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py[430-513] - moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[176-208] - moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[682-729] ### Suggested fix 1. In `build_default_activity_catalog()` add `TemporalActivityDefinition(...)` entries for: - `integration.jules.list_activities` - `integration.jules.answer_question` - `integration.jules.get_auto_answer_config` Use `cfg.activity_integrations_task_queue`, `INTEGRATIONS_FLEET`, and appropriate timeouts/retries. 2. Ensure `_ACTIVITY_HANDLER_ATTRS` already maps these activity types to runtime handlers (it does in this PR). ``` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools

5\. List_activities payload mismatch 🐞 Bug ✓ Correctness 
MoonMind.AgentRun calls integration.jules.list_activities with a raw string session id, but the
TemporalActivityRuntime handler signature is keyword-only session_id, and the activity wrapper
only expands mappings into kwargs; this results in a TypeError (missing required session_id) at
runtime.
Agent Prompt ```` ### Issue description `workflow.execute_activity("integration.jules.list_activities", self.run_id, ...)` passes a string. The runtime activity wrapper only expands `Mapping` inputs into keyword args; otherwise it forwards `request=`. Because `integration_jules_list_activities()` requires `session_id` as a keyword-only arg, this call will raise a `TypeError`. ### Issue Context This affects the integrations worker runtime path (the one actually registered by `worker_entrypoint.py`). ### Fix Focus Areas - moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[705-712] - moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1918-1931] ### Suggested fix Change the workflow invocation to pass a JSON object: ```py activities_result = await workflow.execute_activity( "integration.jules.list_activities", {"session_id": self.run_id}, ... ) ``` Optionally make the runtime handler accept a positional `request` and coerce it, but aligning on mapping payloads is consistent with other integration activities (e.g., `integration.jules.status`). ```` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools

6\. Sends prompt not answer 🐞 Bug ✓ Correctness 
integration.jules.answer_question constructs an LLM instruction prompt and sends it to Jules as the
"answer" without performing any LLM dispatch, so Jules receives meta-instructions rather than an
actual clarification response.
Agent Prompt ``` ### Issue description The auto-answer path does not actually generate an answer; it sends an LLM instruction prompt back to Jules. This is not a clarification response and can cause Jules to re-ask or mis-handle the message. ### Issue Context Spec requires: extract question → dispatch to LLM → send LLM-generated answer via `integration.jules.send_message`. ### Fix Focus Areas - moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py[717-753] - moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py[1932-1975] - specs/094-jules-auto-answer/spec.md[95-106] ### Suggested fix Option A (preferred): move orchestration into workflow 1. Keep `integration.jules.list_activities`. 2. In `agent_run.py`, call `llm` activity (e.g., `mm.activity.llm.complete` / your canonical LLM activity type) with the built prompt. 3. Call existing `integration.jules.send_message` with the LLM output. 4. Remove or simplify `integration.jules.answer_question`. Option B: keep `integration.jules.answer_question` but have it call the LLM activity internally (requires adding LLM capability/route, and will couple integrations worker to LLM worker). Also ensure the content sent to Jules is *only the final answer*, not the meta prompt. ``` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools

7\. Escalation returns non-terminal 🐞 Bug ⛯ Reliability 
When auto-answer is disabled or the max answer count is exceeded, AgentRun sets run_status to
intervention_requested and breaks out of the polling loop, but then still fetches and returns an
AgentRunResult even though the external task is not terminal, effectively completing the workflow
without actually surfacing an intervention state.

Treat `intervention_requested` as terminal for `AgentRun` and return an `AgentRunResult` with a clear failure class (or a dedicated field) that parent workflows/UI interpret as requiring human action. 2. Do not `break`; instead keep polling but keep `run_status` as `intervention_requested` and *skip* auto-answer.
